title: compose: fix build tags
body:
Compose requires running against a socket.  For native Linux clients
(!remote), we can talk directly to the local Podman socket.  For remote
clients, we need to run against podman-machine which is only available
on amd64 and arm64.

[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED] as there is no functional change.

Fixes: #21757

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
Enable the `podman compose` command on all architectures beyond ARM64 and AMD64.
```

This will not build, machine code only works on `amd64 || arm64` so you have to split all the calls into machine code out to make this work.

> This will not build, machine code only works on `amd64 || arm64` so you have to split all the calls into machine code out to make this work.

That makes perfect sense - especially with more caffeine in my system.

> I don't understand this? We can just use the system connection there is no reason to force using a machine for compose at all.

Are you sure?  I believed we need to talk to a UNIX socket.

> > I don't understand this? We can just use the system connection there is no reason to force using a machine for compose at all.
> 
> Are you sure? I believed we need to talk to a UNIX socket.

https://docs.docker.com/engine/security/protect-access/

ssh URL should work but they require docker to be installed on the remote target which is not the case for podman machine right now so yeah for machine it will need the unix socket logic that is true.
But what I really meant by that is that requiring machine for podman-remote compose makes no sense, it can and should still use the configured connections when available.

> But what I really meant by that is that requiring machine for podman-remote compose makes no sense, it can and should still use the configured connections when available.

It would work with the local UNIX socket only then.  But if the default connection is set to some other SSH endpoint, it would fail, wouldn't it?

Can we defer addressing this to another PR or issue?  I am short on time as I will be on PTO starting tomorrow.

> > use the configured connections when available.
> 
> It would work with the local UNIX socket only then. But if the default connection is set to some other SSH endpoint, it would fail, wouldn't it?

Not when the remote host has docker installed or docker symlinked to podman or a connection does not have to use ssh, we also support tcp:// and unix:// url and they should work without any limitations.
